data.description:
## Context

Parked from the brainstorm on the migration-pattern epic (odd-vortex-elbow). `rebar
doctor` is a full **diagnose-and-heal health command** in the `brew doctor` /
`flutter doctor` family â€” a superset of the migration/ensure work. It **depends on**
that migration epic: the ensure-registry + version-marker built there become
doctor's auto-heal category. Design it properly once the migration mechanism exists.

Purpose (per the requesting session): diagnose mismatches between observed project
state and the reported migration version/state; re-apply migrations / correct
inconsistencies that can be **reliably** auto-healed; verify config consistency, that
the ticket store isn't corrupt, and that the environment has the dependencies at the
versions rebar needs; and surface detailed troubleshooting + suggested next steps for
anything it cannot safely auto-heal.

## Proposed design (from OSS research)

**Architecture â€” a registry of check objects.** Each check:
`{ name, category, run() -> {status, message, fix_command?}, is_required, auto_heal? }`.
Run with **crash-isolation** (one check raising must not abort the run â€” flutter's
`ValidationResult.crash`) and **per-check timeouts** on anything touching
network/auth. Slow checks run last.

**Four check categories:**
1. **migration / ensure drift** (auto-heal) â€” the ensure-registry from the migration
   epic; observed-state-vs-marker drift; re-applies idempotent ensures. THIS is the
   provably-safe auto-heal class.
2. **store integrity** (report) â€” reuse fsck's 5 checks (JSON validity, CREATE
   presence, snapshot consistency, orphan events, push-pending). Non-destructive.
3. **config consistency** (advise / selective-heal) â€” rebar's own config valid +
   coherent; foreign config only flagged, never silently rewritten.
4. **environment & dependencies** (advise) â€” Python >=3.11, git present, the
   `[agents]` extra + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (LLM ops), `acli` (live Jira), pre-commit
   hook wired, and the **"stale global build vs repo venv"** foot-gun called out in
   CLAUDE.local.md (`which rebar` resolves to `.venv/bin/rebar`).

**Diagnose-vs-autoheal rule (converged prior art):** auto-heal a finding ONLY if it is
idempotent + reversible + non-destructive + reliably resolves the state; otherwise
print the exact remediation command + docs link and leave it. rebar's ensures satisfy
this by construction; env/config issues generally do not -> advise.

**Severity + exit contract:** adopt chezmoi's tiers `ok / info / warning / error /
failed` (+ a `healed` result for was-broken-now-fixed). **Exit code keys on the fatal
tier only** â€” warnings never fail the process (this cures brew doctor's most-cited
flaw). Standard: `0` = clean or warnings-only or all-healed; non-zero = a fatal check
remains after healing. Offer `--check`/`--dry-run` (read-only, exit non-zero if
anything is behind/broken â€” the terraform `fmt -check` CI-gate pattern) and `--json`
for machine output (test the exit contract; don't let `--json` silently force 0, gh's
bug; don't let an auto-heal reset the failure count, eslint's `--fix` bug).

**Relationship to `upgrade`:** likely `upgrade` = doctor scoped to just the
migration/ensure category (apply pending migrations without the full env/config scan),
or a thin alias â€” decide when designing.

## Gotchas to design against (ranked, from research)
1. Crying wolf / false positives (brew's reputation) -> severity tiers + ignorable
   non-failing warnings + a per-finding "why this might be fine" note.
2. Wrong exit-code contract (gh#8845 exit-0-on-fail; brew exit-1-on-warning; eslint
   autofix resets exit) -> decide it explicitly and TEST it; gate on fatal only.
3. Network/auth checks that hang (flutter license/proxy; npm ping) -> timeout every
   one; classify timeout distinctly from broken; make them skippable.
4. Non-idempotent/destructive auto-fixes (git `gc --prune=now`; docker `prune -a`)
   -> only auto-heal the safe class; rescue-before-delete; default to report/dry-run.
5. "Fix" that only prints links (RN `--fix`) -> don't market advise-only as autofix.
6. Prerequisite/ordering cascades (docker DDxxxx) -> short-circuit dependents on a
   failed root check instead of emitting a confusing cascade.

Prior art surveyed: brew doctor, flutter doctor, react-native doctor (richest
per-check autofix model â€” `HealthCheckInterface` with `getDiagnostics` +
`runAutomaticFix`), npm doctor, yarn doctor, gh auth status, rustup (cautionary â€” no
doctor exists), docker diagnose, git fsck vs git maintenance (sharpest report-vs-mutate
line), terraform validate/fmt, chezmoi doctor (best severity vocabulary).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar doctor` runs a registry of category-tagged checks with crash-isolation and per-check timeouts
- [ ] Auto-heals only the provably-safe class (the migration ensure-registry); advises everything else with an exact next-step command
- [ ] Severity tiers (ok/info/warning/error/failed + healed); exit code gates on the fatal tier only; warnings never fail; behavior is tested
- [ ] `--check`/`--dry-run` read-only mode (CI gate) and `--json` machine output, both with a tested exit-code contract
- [ ] All four categories covered: migration drift (auto-heal), store integrity (reuse fsck), config consistency, environment/dependencies
- [ ] Resolve `upgrade` command relationship (alias vs migration-only subset)
- [ ] Depends on the migration-pattern epic (ensure-registry + version marker) as the auto-heal substrate
data.title: rebar doctor: diagnose-and-heal health command (config + store + env + migration drift)
